monday.description: Build clean coordination between your fretting and picking hands. Speed comes from accuracy and relaxation, not force.

exercises.name: 1-2-3-4 Chromatic Walk
exercises.tips: Keep all fingers close to the fretboard. If notes blur together, slow down immediately.

exercises.name: Speed Bursts
exercises.description: Alternate between slow, controlled playing and brief faster bursts using a simple chromatic pattern. The goal is to experience speed without tension.
exercises.tips: Fast sections should feel controlled, not frantic. Reduce tempo if tension appears.

tuesday.description: Improve accuracy and control when moving across non-adjacent strings.

exercises.name: Adjacent String Skips
exercises.tips: Avoid accidentally hitting skipped strings. Keep movements small and deliberate.

exercises.name: Wide String Skips
exercises.description: Increase difficulty by skipping over multiple strings in a single motion.
exercises.tips: Accuracy first. Listen carefully for unwanted string noise.

wednesday.description: Develop consistency and control in how each note is articulated.

exercises.name: Single-String Control
exercises.description:
Isolate articulation by removing string changes.

```
E|-5-5-5-5-6-6-6-6-7-7-7-7-
```
exercises.tips: All notes should sound even in volume and tone.

exercises.name: Accent Control
exercises.description: Play groups of four notes, emphasizing only the first note of each group.
exercises.tips: Accents should be deliberate. Don’t rush when emphasizing notes.

thursday.description: Improve reach, independence, and comfort in the fretting hand.

exercises.name: The Spider
exercises.tips: Stay relaxed. This exercise should never feel painful.

exercises.name: Four-Finger Linear Stretch
exercises.description: Stretch across frets on a single string using all four fingers (e.g., 5–7–8–10).
exercises.tips: Only stretch as far as comfortable. Avoid forcing reach.

friday.description: Train clean position shifts and confident note placement.

exercises.name: Shift and Land
exercises.description:
Shift between distant frets and land directly on the target.

```
A|-5-----9-----5-----9-
```
exercises.tips: Avoid sliding unless intentional. Land cleanly and in time.

exercises.name: Target Note Jumps
exercises.description: Alternate between two far-apart frets (e.g., 5 and 12) on the same string.
exercises.tips: Visualize the target before moving. Avoid micro-adjustments.

saturday.description: Develop internal time and rhythmic consistency.

exercises.name: Subdivision Switching
exercises.description: Play quarter notes, then eighths, then triplets while keeping the same tempo.
exercises.tips: Count internally. The metronome tempo does not change.

exercises.name: Note Length Control
exercises.description: Alternate between long, sustained notes and short, muted notes.
exercises.tips: Pay attention to clean note endings.

sunday.description: Practice smooth chord changes and continuous musical movement.

exercises.name: Common Chord Changes
exercises.description: Switch slowly and evenly between two common chords (e.g., G to C).
exercises.tips: Prepare fingers early and keep transitions in time.

exercises.name: Continuous Progressions
exercises.description: Loop a 3–4 chord progression continuously without stopping.
exercises.tips: Focus on flow rather than perfection.
